mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion, reducing recession risk and supporting equity valuations through higher expected corporate earnings and lower equity risk premiums over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2013 payroll level at 138,265k suggests ongoing post-crisis recovery
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market normalization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in less than 55% of months where PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 4.94% represents a restrictive monetary stance relative to inflation expectations. A sustained level above 4.5% signals monetary tightening that increases discount rates on future equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated real rates
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of periods where FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts measured by HOUST and retail sales measured by RSAFS jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, the combined demand impulse produces positive equity returns over 20 trading days through higher expected corporate revenues and profit margins.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both increase month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,331k and RSAFS at 380,230M as of February 2013
regime_hint: demand-led expansion with synchronized consumer and housing activity
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in < 50% of instances where both HOUST and RSAFS grow month-over-month, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production measured by INDPRO reflects real economic capacity utilization. A 3-month declining trend in INDPRO signals weakening supply-side momentum and potential demand destruction, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in < 52% of instances where INDPRO shows 3-month decline, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate at 4.7% combined with month-over-month payroll growth creates a tight labor market condition. When UNRATE falls below 5.0% while PAYEMS grows, wage pressure and labor cost inflation accelerate, reducing corporate profit margins and producing negative equity returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.7% and PAYEMS at 138,265k as of March 2013
regime_hint: tight labor market with wage inflation risk
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in > 55% of instances where UNRATE < 5.0% and PAYEMS growth > 100k, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation measured by CPIAUCSL at 207.738 combined with elevated federal funds rate signals monetary policy is restrictive relative to inflation. When CPI growth accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains elevated, real rates compress equity valuations, producing negative 20-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 207.738 and FEDFUNDS at 4.94% as of March 2013
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in > 55% of instances where both conditions are met, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured by GDPC1 is the broadest demand signal. When quarterly GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5% annualized, it signals sustainable economic expansion that supports equity valuations through higher corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11,520.1 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q4 2012
regime_hint: sustained economic expansion above trend growth
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in < 55% of quarters where GDPC1 growth > 2.5% annualized, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production INDPRO combined with payroll employment PAYEMS jointly measure productive capacity and labor utilization. When both series show synchronized month-over-month growth, the supply-side expansion signal produces positive equity returns over 20 trading days through improved unit labor costs and profit margins.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and PAYEMS both increase month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 114.4254 and PAYEMS at 138,265k as of February-March 2013
regime_hint: supply-side expansion with synchronized productivity and employment growth
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in < 52% of instances where both INDPRO and PAYEMS grow month-over-month, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over any 2-year rolling window.
